title: Our Partners - 211info
text: Building and sustaining community partnerships is crucial to the 211info mission. Partnerships that address health and social care navigation have the power to support individual resilience and community health. Community partners assist by funding locally based 211info Community Engagement Coordinators , who conduct outreach presentations, attend community meetings, and work with social service providers to ensure that our database meets local needs. Partner Resources Local and State Partners Care Oregon City of Portland Clackamas Fire District Clark County Columbia Pacific CCO Eastern Oregon CCO InterCommunity Health Network CCO Jackson Care Connect Health Share of Oregon Homeless Services Department (HSD) Multnomah County Oregon Department of Agriculture Oregon Department of Early Learning and Care Oregon Department of Human Services Oregon Health Authority Oregon Housing and Community Services Oregon Office of Emergency Management Oregon State Treasury Portland General Electric Trillium Community Health Plan Umpqua Health Unite Us Washington 211 Washington County Connect Oregon 211info is also a member of Connect Oregon, a coordinated care network of health and social service providers serving Oregon. As a Connect Oregon partner, 211info is connected with other partners in the network through the Unite Us shared technology platform, which enables us to send and receive electronic referrals, address people’s social needs, and improve health across Oregon communities. Learn more about Connect Oregon. At 211info we’re all about looking for the helpers -- and all about connecting members of our community with that help for free. That’s why we work side-by-side with community partners to connect Oregonians with resources when they need them the most. - Dan Herman, 211info CEO
